title: ClockShark vs Deputy for Non-technical users
meta_description: See why Deputy fails first when tracking time requires managing shift scheduling systems instead of simple job-based tracking and why ClockShark is the better pick for non-technical users.
constraint_lens: Non-technical users need tools that are simple to use and avoid complex systems that can be misconfigured.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for non-technical users who need simple job-based tracking for field crews.
one_second_verdict.reason: Deputy fails first because it requires managing shift scheduling systems instead of simple job-based tracking.

verdict.summary: ClockShark is the better choice when you need straightforward job-based tracking for field crews. It focuses on tracking time against jobs without requiring complex scheduling systems. Deputy is built around shift scheduling, which introduces additional setup and configuration that can be confusing and easy to misconfigure.
verdict.decision_rule: If tracking time requires managing shift scheduling systems instead of simple job-based tracking, Deputy fails first.

sections.heading: Why ClockShark fits this non-technical user better
sections.content: This user wants a system that is hard to mess up. ClockShark supports this by focusing on simple job-based tracking without requiring configuration of shift schedules or complex workflows.

bullets.point: ClockShark tracks time directly against jobs and tasks.
bullets.why_it_matters: You can assign work without setting up scheduling systems.

bullets.point: The interface avoids complex shift planning workflows.
bullets.why_it_matters: This reduces the risk of misconfiguration.

bullets.point: Tracking is designed for field crews with simple job selection.
bullets.why_it_matters: This makes it easy to use in real-world work scenarios.

bullets.point: Deputy is built around shift scheduling and workforce planning.
bullets.why_it_matters: This supports structured scheduling, but adds complexity.

bullets.point: Time tracking is tied to scheduled shifts and rosters.
bullets.why_it_matters: This ensures alignment with schedules, but requires setup.

bullets.point: The system includes workforce management features beyond tracking.
bullets.why_it_matters: This increases capability, but introduces more ways to misconfigure.

sections.heading: Where each tool can break down

items.fails_when: You need advanced shift scheduling and workforce planning features.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Deputy if you need structured scheduling systems.

items.fails_when: You just need to track field work but are forced to manage shift schedules and configurations.
items.what_to_do_instead: Switch to ClockShark for simple job-based tracking.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: This can flip if the user needs structured shift scheduling and workforce planning alongside time tracking. In that case, Deputy may be more suitable.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: Choose ClockShark if you want job-based tracking.
Choose Deputy if you need shift scheduling.
If you want simplicity, use ClockShark.

faqs.q: Why is ClockShark better for field crews?
faqs.a: Because it focuses on simple job-based tracking without requiring scheduling systems.

faqs.q: What makes Deputy harder to use?
faqs.a: It requires managing shift schedules and workforce planning systems.
Yes, but that power comes with added complexity.

faqs.q: What is the best tool for simple field work tracking?
faqs.a: ClockShark is better because it keeps tracking straightforward and easy to manage.
